rulings.Case Title: KELLY HOULIHAN VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION STATE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Central Striping Service, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Responses To Admission/Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 27, 2012, Line 1, Defendant Central Striping Service, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Responses To Admission/Interrogatories. Hearing required per the request of the plaintiff to contest the tentative ruling. The tentative ruling (modified to take into account that the hearing is after the ordered compliance date) is that the court adopts the recommendation of Judge Pro Tem Paul Renne as follows: Defendant Central Stripping Services' Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Admission 5, 6, 7, 8 & 10 is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to serve no later than October 4, 2012 his response to Requests for Admission 5, 6, 7, 8, & 10. If a response to any such Request is anything other than an unqualified admission, Plaintiff will provide with specificity the information required by Form Interrogatory no. 17.1 as to such request. Plaintiff's initial responses to the form interrogatories were not timely, thus all objections are waived. Plaintiff and his counsel will pay to Defendant Central Stripping Services sanctions in the amount of $ 2,500.00. The court adds a due date of October 12, 2012 for payment. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 27, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT FRANK EHRET'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Granted without leave to amend as to plaintiff Osiris Asset Management, LLC and granted with twenty days leave to amend as to the individual plaintiffs to add allegations regarding the LLC's assignment of its assets to the individuals and/or facts showing that the $250,000 payment originated with the individuals. rulings.Case Title: JOHN POPPIN VS. GEORGE V CRESSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 27, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF JOHN POPPIN Notice Of Motion For Protective Order; Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Contesting Application For Good Faith Settlement Determination
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 27, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT RONALD HUDSON'S Motion Contesting Application For Good Faith Settlement Determination. The motion to contest is granted. Assuming without deciding that a settlement of a separate and unrelated action between Mr. Vennekolt and CAP can be confirmed in good faith to bar indemnity claims in this action, the settlement fails under Tech Built because there in evidence that the settlement consideration reflects Mr. Vennekolt's proportionate liability in this action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON & REES LLP VS. STEVEN ANTHONY LOPEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 27, 2012, Line 9, DEFENDANTS ERIC KOEHLER and STEVEN LOPEZ' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted with fifteen days leave to amend as to the attorney's fees allegations and denied as to the remainder of the motion. Plaintiff is granted leave to allege a basis for attorney's fees. Defendants' remaining arguments fail because they raise factual issues not amenable to resolution on a motion to strike. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the moving party without need for any appearances. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON & REES LLP VS. STEVEN ANTHONY LOPEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 27, 2012, Line 9, DEFENDANTS ERIC KOEHLER and STEVEN LOPEZ' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. Plaintiff adequately alleges the breach of contract and negligent misrepresentation causes of action. The face of the complaint does not affirmatively and necessarily show that any of the claims are time barred. Whether plaintiff and Mr. Koehler entered into a written fee agreement raises a factual issue that cannot be resolved on demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the moving party without need for any appearances. =(302/HEK)
